steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Find angle BAC from angle sum
steps.content:
In triangle $ABC$, angle $B=90^\circ$ and angle $ACB=58^\circ$. Using the angle sum property, the three angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$:
$$\angle BAC = 180^\circ - 90^\circ - 58^\circ = 32^\circ$$

steps.title: Use similar triangles to find angle CRS
steps.content:
Since $RS$ is parallel to $AB$, triangle $CRS$ is similar to triangle $CAB$ (they share angle $C$, and the parallel condition makes the other angles match). In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal:
$$\angle CRS = \angle CAB = 32^\circ$$

steps.title: Find angle ARS using supplementary angles
steps.content:
Point $R$ lies on segment $AC$, so angles $CRS$ and $ARS$ form a linear pair at $R$. Linear-pair angles are supplementary:
$$\angle ARS = 180^\circ - \angle CRS = 180^\circ - 32^\circ = 148^\circ$$
The answer is 148.
